assessments.status_rationale: Today’s coverage describes a fragile U.S.-Iran cease-fire and disputes (including over Lebanon) and even accusations of breaches, but does not report a public announcement that explicitly extends the ceasefire beyond an initial two-week period. Deadline is still upcoming (2026-04-22).
No reporting in today’s bullets about House Appropriations Committee releasing a FY2027 DHS appropriations proposal or CISA funding levels; deadline is 2026-07-31.
No OFAC designation described in today’s bullets; deadline is 2026-12-31.
Today’s bullets note oil prices resuming gains amid ceasefire breach accusations, but do not provide EIA DCOILBRENTEU prints or confirm a day at or below $85.00; deadline is 2026-06-30.

A CNBC item discusses saving money on flights as airlines raise prices, but it does not clearly report first checked-bag fee increases (or identify at least two named major airlines doing so) relative to 2026-03-31; deadline is 2026-09-30.

Coverage concerns Anthropic’s court loss related to a Pentagon 'supply chain risk' label/blacklisting; nothing indicates release of a cybersecurity-focused 'Mythos' model for general commercial use; deadline is 2026-08-31.
No mention in today’s bullets of CISA/DOE CESER confirming an Iran-linked cyber incident causing ≥4 hours of operational disruption at U.S. critical infrastructure; deadline is 2026-09-30.
No DOJ announcement in today’s bullets about a tariff-evasion criminal case exceeding $50 million in attempted/evaded duties; deadline is 2026-12-31.
No reporting in today’s bullets about a newly posted Phase 2 (or 2/3) ClinicalTrials.gov trial for a reversible male contraceptive with planned enrollment ≥100; deadline is 2026-12-31.
